title: refactor: move Enter UI onto shared package
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Moves Enter from duplicated local UI primitives, fonts, tokens, and app styles to `@pollinations_ai/ui`.
- Adds the shared primitives and modules Enter now consumes: dialogs, dropdowns, sliders, multi-selects, period picker, scroll area, auth modal shell, modality helpers, and wallet components.
- Keeps Enter product behavior in Enter, including dashboard navigation and centered modal flows for API key and app key creation.
- Defines the package boundary around `@pollinations_ai/ui`, `/auth`, `/wallet`, `/modality`, `/showcase`, package CSS/font exports, and colorable source SVGs under `@pollinations_ai/ui/assets/*`.
- Keeps favicon, PWA, and SEO assets app-owned in Enter, and removes unused public logo/Myceli/stats assets.
- Updates the BYOP React demo to React 19 and the current public UI package API.

Validation:
- `npm run test --prefix packages/ui -- Dialog`
- `npm run build --prefix packages/ui`
- `npm run build:ui --prefix enter.pollinations.ai`
- `tsc --noEmit -p enter.pollinations.ai/tsconfig.json`
- `npm run build:frontend --prefix enter.pollinations.ai`

gist.headline: Packing up the UI
gist.blurb: We just did some major spring cleaning in the codebase. All our UI components—buttons, dialogs, sliders—now live in a shared package, making it way easier to build consistent apps.
gist.summary: Migrated UI primitives, fonts, and design tokens from the Enter dashboard into a standalone @pollinations_ai/ui package.
gist.impact: Internal development gets a lot cleaner, as all apps can now pull standardized components like dialogs and sliders from a single source of truth.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the monitor robot organizing UI elements (buttons, sliders) into neat storage boxes or shelves, representing the move to a shared UI package.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny tool belt, organizing glowing UI components like buttons and sliders into neat wooden cubbies. A chunky monitor-headed robot helps by carrying a box of pixelated fonts. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi Stardew Valley aesthetic, emotionally warm.
